raw_consumer_statement:
On XX/XX/year>, I purchased an airline ticket from the merchant, XXXX, for {$720.00}. I received an order confirmation and e-ticket via email. 

In XX/XX/year>, I contacted the merchant through their online chat to request a cancellation. I was informed of a 'cancellation fee ' that was greater than the total value of my ticket, making any refund impossible. This exorbitant fee was not disclosed at any point during the purchase process. 

On XX/XX/year>, I submitted a dispute with AMEX for this charge, as the service was not rendered and the cancellation terms were misrepresented. 

On XX/XX/year>, I again formally requested that the merchant cancel the ticket, communicating this through both online chat and email to ensure there was a record. 

On XX/XX/year>, AMEX informed me that they had resolved the dispute in the merchant 's favor. AMEX stated that the merchant provided evidence that the 'terms and conditions of the purchase were clearly disclosed both verbally and in written form. ' This is factually incorrect and is the basis of my complaint against both AMEX and XXXX. AMEX accepted the merchant 's claims without proper scrutiny and has since refused to accept any follow-up communication regarding this dispute. 

Refuting the Merchant 's 'Evidence ' : The merchant 's claim of 'verbal disclosure ' is false, as I never had a phone call with them. All communication was via email and online chat. Furthermore, the merchant submitted 'terms and conditions ' and a 'purchase form ' to AMEX as evidence. I attest that I never saw these specific terms at the time of purchase, and the form they provided to AMEX is different from the checkout screen I used. The excessive refund fee was not disclosed. The attached confirmation email from the merchant also contains no links to or attachments of these terms. 

Refuting the Merchant 's 'Process ' : The merchant wrongly implies I failed to follow their cancellation process. My attached email thread clearly shows I made an unambiguous request to cancel. The merchant refused to honor this request and instead insisted I sign additional forms and agree to new terms and conditions. Due to the deceptive nature of the hidden fees, I did not trust their process and did not sign these additional forms. My intention to cancel was made perfectly clear.
